description: Fix ToolError misuse in add_reaction, URL-encode file paths in contents endpoints, and fix Retry-After header parsing in the GitHub plugin.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/github && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Use ToolError.validation instead of plain Error in add_reaction tool
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the add_reaction tool to throw ToolError.validation instead of a plain Error so that the MCP dispatch chain properly categorizes the error.
acceptanceCriteria: add-reaction.ts line 36 throws ToolError.validation('Either issue_number or comment_id must be provided') instead of new Error(...)
ToolError is imported from @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk in add-reaction.ts
cd plugins/github && npm run build passes
cd plugins/github && npm run type-check passes
cd plugins/github && npm run lint passes
cd plugins/github && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: plugins/github/src/tools/add-reaction.ts

Line 36: `throw new Error('Either issue_number or comment_id must be provided');`
Change to: `throw ToolError.validation('Either issue_number or comment_id must be provided');`

Add `ToolError` to the import from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk' at line 1 (currently only imports `defineTool`).

userStories.title: URL-encode file paths in GitHub contents endpoints
userStories.description: As a user, I want to read and write files with spaces or special characters in their paths so that the GitHub API receives correctly encoded URLs.
acceptanceCriteria: get-file-content.ts URL-encodes each path segment of params.path before interpolating into the endpoint URL
create-or-update-file.ts URL-encodes each path segment of params.path before interpolating into the endpoint URL
Forward slashes in the path are NOT encoded (they are path separators)
Paths without special characters continue to work identically
cd plugins/github && npm run build passes
cd plugins/github && npm run type-check passes
cd plugins/github && npm run lint passes
cd plugins/github && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/github/src/tools/get-file-content.ts line 26
- plugins/github/src/tools/create-or-update-file.ts line 37

Both interpolate `params.path` directly into the URL:
```
`/repos/${params.owner}/${params.repo}/contents/${params.path}`
```

The fix must URL-encode each segment of the path while preserving forward slashes as path separators. The correct approach is to split on '/', encode each segment, and rejoin:
```
const encodedPath = params.path.split('/').map(encodeURIComponent).join('/');
```

Then use `encodedPath` in the template literal.

Do NOT encode `params.owner` or `params.repo` — GitHub usernames and repo names cannot contain URL-unsafe characters.

Do NOT use a single `encodeURIComponent(params.path)` — that would encode the forward slashes too, breaking the URL entirely.

userStories.title: Use SDK parseRetryAfterMs in GitHub API callers
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the GitHub plugin to correctly parse Retry-After headers in both seconds and HTTP-date formats so that AI agents receive valid retry timing metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: Both api() (line ~114) and apiRaw() (line ~175) in github-api.ts use parseRetryAfterMs from @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk instead of `Number(retryAfter) * 1000`
The import for parseRetryAfterMs is added to the existing @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk import line
HTTP-date format Retry-After values are correctly parsed instead of producing NaN
cd plugins/github && npm run build passes
cd plugins/github && npm run type-check passes
cd plugins/github && npm run lint passes
cd plugins/github && npm run format:check passes
